fs.writeFileSync(tmpPath, JSON.stringify(awInfo, null, 2));
console.log('Generated aw_info.json at:', tmpPath);

core.setOutput('model', awInfo.model);

2026-02-26T17:38:52.9240883Z Created an issue documenting all 10 inconsistencies found across 6 files in `documentation/`. Key findings:
2026-02-26T17:38:52.9241481Z 
2026-02-26T17:38:54.3675611Z 1. **`Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md`** — Extensive stale Mocha/Chai patterns: `assert` from `"chai"`, `Context` from `"mocha"`, `new Recorder(this.currentTest)`, `this`-bound `beforeEach`, and relative imports missing `.js` extensions. All should use Vitest patterns.
2026-02-26T17:38:54.3677327Z 
2026-02-26T17:38:55.2001747Z 2. **`steps-after-generations.md`** (lines 70, 79, 85) — `pnpm build --filter=${PACKAGE_NAME}` missing the critical trailing `...`, which causes builds to fail when dependencies haven't been built.
2026-02-26T17:38:55.2003186Z 
2026-02-26T17:38:55.4837643Z 3. **`RLC-Swagger-quickstart.md`** (line 112) — `pnpm update` should be `pnpm install`.
2026-02-26T17:38:55.4838164Z 
2026-02-26T17:38:56.8484637Z 4. **`MIGRATION-guide-for-next-generation-management-libraries.md`** (line 44) — `clientSecrat` typo should be `clientSecret`.
2026-02-26T17:38:56.8485447Z 
2026-02-26T17:38:57.1317975Z 5. **`next-generation-quickstart.md`** (line 6) — `` `azure/arm-resources` `` missing the `@` scope prefix.
2026-02-26T17:38:57.1319014Z 
2026-02-26T17:38:57.3726438Z 6. **`resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md`** (line 16) — The `git add` one-liner is missing a `/` separator, producing an invalid path.

This reflects status of builds and checks.\n 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"minimal_output":{"default":true,"description":"Return minimal repository information (default: true). 2026-02-26T17:39:12.5618291Z {"timestamp":"2026-02-26T17:35:20.048684689Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"message":"Documentation analysis complete. 2026-02-26T17:39:12.5620169Z {"timestamp":"2026-02-26T17:35:20.053512992Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"safeoutputs","payload":{"id":3,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"result\":\"success\"}","type":"text"}],"isError":false}}}
2026-02-26T17:39:12.5651217Z {"timestamp":"2026-02-26T17:38:50.044774598Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"This issue tracks inconsistencies found across the `documentation/` folder after comparing each file against the current source code, tooling, and repository structure.\n\n---\n\n## 1. `documentation/Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md` — Stale Mocha/Chai patterns throughout\n\nThe code examples in this file still use Mocha and Chai, but the repository has fully migrated to Vitest. Actual test files (e.g. `sdk/purview/purview-workflow-rest/test/public/workflow.spec.ts`, `sdk/monitor/arm-monitor/test/monitor.spec.ts`) show the correct patterns.\n\n### 1a. `import { assert } from \"chai\"` (lines 373 and 487)\n\n**Current (incorrect):**\n```typescript\nimport { assert } from \"chai\";\n```\n**Should be:**\n```typescript\nimport { describe, it, assert, beforeEach, afterEach } from \"vitest\";\n```\n`chai` is no longer a dependency; `assert` is imported directly from `vitest`.\n\n---\n\n### 1b. `import { Context } from \"mocha\"` (lines 418 and 488)\n\n**Current (incorrect):**\n```typescript\nimport { Context } from \"mocha\";\n```\n**Should be removed.** Mocha is no longer used. Vitest passes a `TaskContext` to each hook via a parameter.\n\n---\n\n### 1c. `new Recorder(context.currentTest)` / `new Recorder(this.currentTest)` (lines 443 and 512)\n\n**Current (incorrect):**\n```typescript\n// line 443\nconst recorder = new Recorder(context.currentTest);\n// line 512\nrecorder = new Recorder(this.currentTest);\n```\n**Should be:**\n```typescript\nrecorder = new Recorder(ctx);  // ctx is the TaskContext from vitest's beforeEach(async (ctx) =\u003e { ... })\n```\n`context.currentTest` and `this.currentTest` are Mocha-only APIs. The current recorder API (v4.x) accepts a Vitest `TaskContext` object directly, as seen in `sdk/purview/purview-workflow-rest/test/public/workflow.spec.ts`:\n```typescript\nbeforeEach(async (ctx) =\u003e {\n  recorder = new Recorder(ctx);\n```\n\n---\n\n### 1d. `beforeEach(async function () { ... }` using `this` binding (lines ~387 and ~510)\n\n**Current (incorrect):**\n```typescript\nbeforeEach(async function () {\n  recorder = await createRecorder(this);\n```\n**Should be:**\n```typescript\nbeforeEach(async (ctx) =\u003e {\n  recorder = await createRecorder(ctx);\n```\n`this` binding is a Mocha pattern. Vitest uses arrow-function hooks with a `ctx` parameter.\n\n---\n\n### 1e. Imports missing `.js` extensions (lines 374–375, 420, 489)\n\n**Current (incorrect):**\n```typescript\nimport { PurviewDataMapClient } from \"../../src\";\nimport { createClient, createRecorder } from \"./utils/recordedClient\";\nimport { MonitorClient } from \"../src/monitorClient\";\n```\n**Should be:**\n```typescript\nimport { PurviewDataMapClient } from \"../../src/index.js\";\nimport { createClient, createRecorder } from \"./utils/recordedClient.js\";\nimport { MonitorClient } from \"../src/monitorClient.js\";\n```\nThe repo uses ESM. All relative imports must include the `.js` extension, as seen in actual test files.\n\n---\n\n## 2. `documentation/steps-after-generations.md` — Missing `...` trailing on build commands (lines 70, 79, 85)\n\nLine 63 correctly shows:\n```shell\npnpm build --filter=${PACKAGE_NAME}...\n```\nBut lines 70, 79, and 85 omit the trailing `...`:\n```shell\npnpm build --filter=${PACKAGE_NAME}\n```\n**Context (lines 68–88):**\n\nThe Windows (`SET`) variant on line 79 and the Linux playback variant on line 85 are both missing the `...` suffix.\n\n**Should be:**\n```shell\npnpm build --filter=${PACKAGE_NAME}...\n```\nThe trailing `...` is critical — it instructs Turborepo to build the package **and all its local dependencies**. Without it, the build fails if dependent packages haven't been built yet. `CONTRIBUTING.md` (line 121) explicitly documents this requirement:\n\u003e \"To ensure that it builds all of its dependencies, you must use the `...` suffix.\"\n\n---\n\n## 3. `documentation/RLC-Swagger-quickstart.md` — `pnpm update` should be `pnpm install` (line 112)\n\n**Current (incorrect):**\n```shell\npnpm update\n```\n**Should be:**\n```shell\npnpm install\n```\n`pnpm update` upgrades packages to the latest allowed versions and modifies `package.json` version constraints — not the intended action here. `pnpm install` restores the dependency tree to match the lockfile without altering constraints.\n\n---\n\n## 4. `documentation/MIGRATION-guide-for-next-generation-management-libraries.md` — Typo `clientSecrat` (line 44)\n\n**Current (incorrect):**\n```typescript\nconst credentials = new ClientSecretCredential(tenantId, clientId, clientSecrat);\n```\n**Should be:**\n```typescript\nconst credentials = new ClientSecretCredential(tenantId, clientId, clientSecret);\n```\n`clientSecrat` is a typo. The variable is correctly declared as `clientSecret` earlier in the same file (line 35: `clientSecret,`). Copy-pasting this snippet as-is would cause a `ReferenceError` at runtime.\n\n---\n\n## 5. `documentation/next-generation-quickstart.md` — Missing `@` scope prefix (line 6)\n\n**Current (incorrect):**\n```\n`azure/arm-resources`, `@azure/arm-storage`,\n```\n**Should be:**\n```\n`@azure/arm-resources`, `@azure/arm-storage`,\n```\nThe npm package is scoped as `@azure/arm-resources`. The missing `@` makes it look like a GitHub repository path rather than an npm package name. The same line correctly uses `@azure/arm-storage`, so this is a simple typo.\n\n---\n\n## 6. `documentation/resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md` — Missing `/` in `git add` path (line 16 shell one-liner)\n\n**Current (incorrect):**\n```shell\ngit checkout upstream/main `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml \u0026\u0026 pnpm install \u0026\u0026 git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`pnpm-lock.yaml\n```\n**Should be:**\n```shell\ngit checkout upstream/main `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml \u0026\u0026 pnpm install \u0026\u0026 git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml\n```\n`git rev-parse --show-toplevel` returns an absolute path without a trailing slash (e.g., `/home/user/azure-sdk-for-js`). The `git checkout` part of the command correctly has a `/` separator, but the `git add` part is missing it, which would produce an invalid path like `/home/user/azure-sdk-for-jspnpm-lock.yaml` and silently fail to stage the file.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| File | Line(s) | Issue |\n|------|---------|-------|\n| `Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md` | 373, 487 | `import { assert } from \"chai\"` → import from `\"vitest\"` |\n| `Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md` | 418, 488 | `import { Context } from \"mocha\"` → remove, use vitest |\n| `Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md` | 443, 512 | `new Recorder(context.currentTest)` / `new Recorder(this.currentTest)` → `new Recorder(ctx)` |\n| `Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md` | ~387, ~510 | `beforeEach(async function () { ... this` → arrow fn with `ctx` param |\n| `Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md` | 374, 375, 420, 489 | Relative imports missing `.js` extension |\n| `steps-after-generations.md` | 70, 79, 85 | `pnpm build --filter=${PACKAGE_NAME}` missing trailing `...` |\n| `RLC-Swagger-quickstart.md` | 112 | `pnpm update` → `pnpm install` |\n| `MIGRATION-guide-for-next-generation-management-libraries.md` | 44 | `clientSecrat` typo → `clientSecret` |\n| `next-generation-quickstart.md` | 6 | `` `azure/arm-resources` `` missing `@` → `` `@azure/arm-resources` `` |\n| `resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md` | 16 | Missing `/` in `git add \\`git rev-parse --show-toplevel\\`pnpm-lock.yaml` |\n","title":"Documentation inconsistencies in `documentation/` folder"},"name":"create_issue"}}}
